stories.title: Agent-Native Immune System: Architecture, Taxonomy, and Engineering
stories.author: Dehui Li
bullet_points: The shift to autonomous, agent-based AI systems introduces a fundamentally expanded threat landscape.
Traditional security measures (e.g., perimeter security, training-time alignment) are insufficient because they operate outside the agent's active reasoning loop.
New vulnerabilities exist at runtime, including memory poisoning, tool-chain manipulation, and multi-agent protocol exploitation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its primary function in NLP.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language model performance.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformers and Llama ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in generative AI tasks.
The model is associated with key technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function in large language model applications.
Key associated technologies include transformers and safetensors, standard components in modern NLP.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon devices.
The performance boost is attributed to updates within the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage for LLMs.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM integration.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI execution speed.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with existing messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is bringing powerful AI capabilities directly onto the user's own hardware.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: China claims the world’s fastest supercomputer
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: China has regained the title of the world's fastest supercomputer, surpassing the previous leader.
This achievement occurred despite ongoing US trade restrictions on high-powered computing components sold to China.
The news highlights a significant technological race and geopolitical tension in high-performance computing.

stories.title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: South Korea is planning a massive military overhaul, integrating drones into its combat strategy.
The entire half-million-strong military force will undergo training focused on drone operation.
Drones are being positioned as a 'universal combat tool' in the nation's defense modernization efforts.

stories.title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The New York Times is reportedly challenging Microsoft's copyright claims related to a supercomputer built for OpenAI.
The shift in legal strategy follows a recent Supreme Court ruling that was unfavorable to Sony in copyright matters.
This signals ongoing, high-stakes legal battles concerning AI training data and copyright infringement in the tech industry.

stories.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool
stories.author: Vincent Cohen-Addad
bullet_points: AI is accelerating scientific discovery, creating a bottleneck in traditional human peer review.
The paper proposes using AI tools, like Google's Paper Assistant, to automate and accelerate the scientific review process.
The work introduces a taxonomy to frame the transition toward AI-driven verification in science.

stories.title: Govern the Repository, Not the Agent: Measuring Ecosystem-Level Risk in AI-Native Software
stories.author: Daniel Russo
bullet_points: Autonomous coding agents are now operating at scale within shared software repositories.
Current evaluation methods focus on individual agent performance, ignoring systemic repository-level risks.
The research introduces 'integration friction' to measure the cumulative problems caused by multiple agent contributions.

stories.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool
stories.author: Vincent Cohen-Addad
bullet_points: AI is accelerating scientific discovery, creating a bottleneck in traditional human peer review.
The paper proposes using AI to automate and accelerate the scientific review and verification process.
It introduces a taxonomy to frame the necessary transition toward AI-assisted scientific validation.

stories.title: Vision-Default, Prior-Override: Causal Mechanisms of Perception-Knowledge Conflict in Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Michal Golovanevsky
bullet_points: Investigates the causal mechanisms by which Vision-Language Models (VLMs) resolve conflicts between visual input and pre-existing world knowledge.
Uses advanced mechanistic interpretability techniques, including activation patching across residual streams, attention heads, and MLP sublayers.
Findings provide a component-level causal account of perception-knowledge conflict, crucial for improving the reliability of multimodal AI systems.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also enables web search functionality within the Claude Code environment.
This enhances the capabilities of AI agents by adding modularity (subagents) and real-time information access (web search).

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: China’s Z.ai claims it can match Mythos on cybersecurity
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: China's Zhipu AI (Z.ai) released its open-weight GLM-5.2 model.
Some research suggests GLM-5.2 matches Mythos in specific cybersecurity and bug-finding tasks.
The development indicates a narrowing capability gap between Chinese AI models and Western leaders like OpenAI/Anthropic in specialized areas.

stories.title: Suno launches Spark incubator program to feed independent artists to its AI machine
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Suno is expanding its strategy beyond being a simple AI music generator.
The new 'Spark' incubator program aims to support and promote independent artists.
The program offers grants, mentorship, and marketing assistance to unsigned musicians.

stories.title: Prosecutors used ChatGPT logs as evidence in the Palisades fire trial
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Prosecutors utilized ChatGPT logs as evidence in a high-profile arson trial.
The case involves a deadly wildfire in Los Angeles, highlighting the intersection of AI and criminal justice.
The use of AI logs alongside traditional evidence (location data, video) signals a growing trend in digital forensics.

stories.title: Antibiotic "megacluster" discovery provides new strategy to fight superbugs
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: A new antibiotic 'megacluster' has been discovered.
This discovery offers a novel strategy against antibiotic-resistant 'superbugs'.
The finding is considered an important step in replenishing the global antibiotic supply.

stories.title: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: DexCompose addresses the challenge of composing individual dexterous manipulation policies into multi-task skills using a single hand.
The core problem addressed is destructive interference caused by conflicting demands on overlapping fingers when adding new tasks.
The proposed solution is a role-aware residual composition framework designed to reuse pretrained dexterous policies effectively.

stories.title: Agentic Hardware Design as Repository-Level Code Evolution
stories.author: Brucek Khailany
bullet_points: Introduces HORIZON, a self-evolving agent framework for hardware design.
Treats hardware design as a process analogous to repository-level code evolution.
Utilizes a Markdown harness and Git operations for state management and evolution within an isolated worktree.

stories.title: Exposure Bias Can Alleviate Itself via Directional and Frequency Rectification in Flow Matching
stories.author: Shao-Lun Huang
bullet_points: The paper addresses exposure bias in Flow Matching (FM) models, a known issue arising from training/inference discrepancies.
It proposes DEFAR (DirEctional-Frequency Adaptive Rectification), a novel framework to self-rectify exposure bias.
The core idea is that the exposure bias itself contains dynamic signals that can be used to guide the rectification process.

stories.title: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: DexCompose addresses the challenge of composing multiple dexterous manipulation tasks using a single hand.
The core problem addressed is destructive interference when adding new tasks to existing manipulation skills due to conflicting finger demands.
The proposed solution is a role-aware residual composition framework designed to reuse pretrained dexterous policies.

stories.title: VGB for Masked Diffusion Model: Efficient Test-time Scaling for Reward Satisfaction and Sample Editing
stories.author: Molei Tao
bullet_points: Introduces MDM-VGB, a new discrete diffusion sampler designed for Masked Diffusion Models (MDM).
Focuses on efficient test-time scaling to ensure generated outputs satisfy structural constraints or optimize downstream rewards.
Augments unmasking generation with a theoretically principled reward-guided remasking technique, inspired by backtracking Markov chains.

stories.title: Democratic ICAI: Debating Our Way to Steering Principles from Preferences
stories.author: Shirish Karande
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in preference-based AI alignment, noting that pairwise labels obscure the underlying reasoning in complex human judgments.
It builds upon Inverse Constitutional AI (ICAI) to enhance decision interpretability by summarizing preferences into natural-language principles.
The research introduces a method to overcome the single-pass explanation limitation of existing ICAI models to capture decision nuance.

stories.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool
stories.author: Vincent Cohen-Addad
bullet_points: The paper addresses the scalability crisis in traditional human peer review due to the rapid increase in AI-assisted scientific output.
It proposes using Artificial Intelligence to accelerate the scientific verification and review process itself.
The work introduces a taxonomy to frame the discussion around this necessary transition in scientific validation.

stories.title: HPRO: Hierarchical Progressive Reward Optimization via Preference Extraction for Emotional Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Xiangmin Xu
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of LLM-based Text-to-Speech (TTS) models converging to statistically averaged prosody, hindering emotional expressiveness.
Proposes HPRO (Hierarchical Progressive Reward Optimization) as a preference-driven optimization method to improve emotional TTS.
Identifies and aims to resolve structural mismatches in existing preference-driven approaches, specifically 'information conflict' between content and emotion gradients.

stories.title: Cognitive Episodes in LLM Reasoning Traces Enable Interpretable Human Item Difficulty Prediction
stories.author: Dawei Zhou
bullet_points: The research proposes a novel method to predict human item difficulty in educational assessments.
Instead of relying solely on text or human calibration, the method uses 'Cognitive Episodes' observed in LLM reasoning traces.
This approach aims to provide interpretable insights into the cognitive burden an item places on a solver.

stories.title: From Tokens to States: LLMs as a Special Case of World Models and the Continuous Path Beyond
stories.author: Paul Dubois
bullet_points: The paper challenges the binary view separating LLMs (token prediction) and World Models (reality simulation).
It posits that LLMs can be viewed as a degenerate special case of World Models, where the state space is defined by token sequences.
The work aligns with arguments (like LeCun's) suggesting that achieving AGI may require moving beyond pure autoregressive token prediction toward latent-space architectures.

stories.title: Mechanism-Driven Monitors for Preemptive Detection of LLM Training Instability
stories.author: Yang Zheng
bullet_points: Addresses the high cost and difficulty of detecting LLM training instability before failure.
Proposes a novel approach using 'mechanism-driven monitors' derived from the functional role of model modules.
Aims to preemptively detect instability, which can persist even when standard loss/gradient metrics appear normal.

stories.title: MultiHashFormer: Hash-based Generative Language Models
stories.author: Nikolaos Aletras
bullet_points: Introduces MultiHashFormer, a new framework for hash-based generative language models.
Addresses the limitation of prior hashing methods (many-to-one collisions) that prevented their use in causal LMs.
Allows for hash-based autoregression by representing each token as a unique hash signature, aiming for parameter efficiency.

stories.title: Can LLMs Judge Better Than They Generate? Evaluating Task Asymmetry, Mechanistic Interpretability and Transferability for In-Context QA
stories.author: Sambaran Bandyopadhyay
bullet_points: Challenges the common assumption that LLM evaluation (LLM-as-a-Judge) is inherently easier than generation.
Tests this asymmetry in a controlled in-context Question Answering (QA) setting, isolating the evaluation task.
Findings show that generation accuracy outperformed self-evaluation on three out of four tested benchmarks.

stories.title: How Three People Built a Working AI Agent in One Night: The Architecture, the Bugs, and What We'd Do Differently
stories.author: sowmya_5ab5a8078a6f2ad464
bullet_points: Details the rapid development of a working multi-agent AI system.
Focuses on the technical aspects, including architecture and persistent memory implementation.
Provides practical insights into the development process, covering bugs and lessons learned.
